column1: # mapping_set_title: 'OCSF to UCO (Unified Cyber Ontology) class mappings'
#   Class-level mappings between OCSF (Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework)
#   and UCO master schema (Unified Cyber Ontology).  UCO is an upper-level
#   cyber observable ontology with very granular sub-typing (e.g. Process /
#   UNIXProcess / WindowsProcess).  Where OCSF has a corresponding
#   platform-specific extension class, the platform-specific UCO class is
#   the exact match; otherwise the abstract UCO class is the exact match.

column8: comment
Both are abstract roots; OCSF is narrower (cybersecurity events/objects), UCO covers the full cyber observable space.
OCSF Observable parallels UCO ObservableObject (a directly-observed cyber artefact).
Direct correspondence — both model a temporal perception of an observable.
Direct correspondence; UCO Process is the abstract parent of OS-specific variants.
OCSF's Linux extension narrows Process to UNIX semantics (uid/gid/etc).
macOS is UNIX-derived; UCO has no separate MacOSProcess class.
Direct correspondence.

Person-as-account view; UCO splits Identity (Person) from UserAccount.
Direct correspondence.
Direct correspondence (RFC 3912 conformant).
OCSF Dns wraps a DNS exchange; UCO DNSRecord models a single resource record.
Each DNS answer is a UCO DNSRecord.
OCSF Fingerprint carries hash algorithm + value pairs equivalent to UCO Hash.
UCO HTTPConnection captures the full HTTP exchange; HttpRequest is one half.
UCO HTTPConnection captures the full HTTP exchange; HttpResponse is one half.
Direct correspondence (cookie metadata).
Direct correspondence.
Same target as the Application event class; the OCSF object is an older form.
Direct correspondence.
Direct correspondence.
OCSF DomainContact captures registrar contact info; UCO DomainName is the closest related term.
OCSF Endpoint is a host with network identity; UCO Device is the closest abstract equivalent.
Direct correspondence.
OCSF narrows to peripherals; UCO Device is the abstract parent.
OCSF Image is closer to "container/disk image" than to a raster picture; mapping is best-effort.
OCSF Service models a network service; UCO has OnlineService for hosted services.
UCO has no first-class WindowsService; AD account is the closest related; consider unmapped.
Direct correspondence.
OCSF LdapPerson maps to UCO Person identity.
Direct correspondence (geospatial location).
UCO Library covers loadable shared libraries; OCSF Module covers loaded modules including DLLs/SOs.
Direct correspondence.
UCO models malicious software via MaliciousTool (sub-class of Tool with a malicious role).
OCSF DataClassification labels data sensitivity; UCO MarkingDefinition is the analogous handling label.
UCO has no Kernel class; OperatingSystem is the closest related concept.
Loadable driver; closest UCO match is Library.
UCO has no Container class; Software is the closest related abstract concept.
Direct correspondence.
